description: JSON receipts from thaw demo runs. Each receipt is the sanitized output of a demo script (see demos/*.py). Re-run the demos to produce your own — numbers are not doctored.
schema_fields: demo: which demo script produced this receipt
trunk_tokens: tokenized length of the shared context
timings_s: measured wall-clocks (load, trunk, fork, fork_completions, etc.)
modes: per-mode elapsed + counts (A native / B fork / C cold)
checks: correctness assertions (non-empty, diverge, prefix cache hit)
samples: up to 3 truncated branch outputs per mode, 200 chars each
pod: GPU name + memory + CUDA version
software: python / vllm / torch / thaw-native versions
git: commit hash of the demo run
reproduce: pip install thaw-native vllm && git clone https://github.com/thaw-ai/thaw && python demos/fork_smoke_test.py --json-out my_receipt.json  # for the cheapest run

receipts.headline: fork handle in 0.289s, 4 divergent same-process completions in 0.633s
receipts.note: Same-process fork primitive. All 4 branches non-empty and divergent; prefix cache enabled.

receipts.headline: 8 candidate branches from an 8k-token trunk; native batch 5.8s vs fork+subprocess 401.5s (workers_s=61.7s, subprocess boot=339.8s)
receipts.note: Fork path pays one-time subprocess cold-start on an 80 GB pod with workers=1. Native and fork produce the same max_pass_rate (0.875). Proves cross-process fork works end-to-end on real H100; fork's latency edge shows up at larger N or under process-isolation constraints, not at this size.

receipts.headline: 16 PPO rollouts from an 8k-token trunk; native batch 5.04s vs fork+subprocess 389.7s vs cold×16 43.2s (extrapolated)
receipts.note: Same reward stats (mean=0.812, max=1.6) across native and fork paths — proves bit-consistent reward signal. Fork beats 16× cold engines (43s) in GPU-hour math at PPO scale; native batch beats everything when the whole batch fits in one engine.

receipts.headline: fork handle in 0.21s, 4 divergent same-process completions in 2.29s
receipts.note: Reconstructed from real run stdout — pod was terminated before the checked-in script existed.

receipts.headline: ForkPool RL shape: init_pool 22.3s amortized over 5 rounds × 4 branches at 0.88s median/round (1.16s first round)
receipts.note: Pre-warmed subprocess workers hold the 8B engine with real weights; each fork snapshots KV only. Per-round cost drops from ~340s cold-boot to sub-second. All rounds 4/4 non-empty and divergent. Requires thaw-native source build until 0.3.1 — PyPI 0.3.0 wheel has a WC-pinned-freeze regression that caps throughput at 50 MB/s.
